target.title: fix(memory-wiki): don't prune unsafe-local pages when a source path is   transiently unavailable
target.body:
Closes #97523

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where memory-wiki users running `vaultMode: "unsafe-local"` would permanently lose imported pages **and their hand-written human-notes blocks** when a configured source path became transiently unavailable (an undocked external drive, a rebooting NAS, or a cloud-synced folder that is not mounted yet). Any `wiki_*` tool that triggered a sync while the path was gone deleted every imported page for that path; when the path came back, pages were recreated empty and the user's notes were unrecoverable.

## Why This Change Was Made

A transiently unreadable source collects zero artifacts in exactly the same way a real deletion does, and the `unsafe-local` sync pruned unconditionally on that signal. The fix makes the prune **source-scope aware**:

- `collectUnsafeLocalArtifacts` now records the set of configured roots that could not be fully read — `fs.stat` failing on the path itself, `fs.readdir` failing on the configured directory or **any nested subdirectory** (recursive readability propagates up), or a missing explicit file.
- `syncMemoryWikiUnsafeLocalSources` preserves imported entries that live under those unreadable roots (keeps their keys active), then prunes normally. So a transient outage never deletes a page or its human-notes, while **readable roots still clean up their genuinely deleted files** — even when another configured source is offline at the same time.

This is the narrowed behavior ClawSweeper recommended ("Scope Pruning Before Merge"): unavailable roots preserve their entries; healthy roots still prune. An explicit configured file that vanishes is indistinguishable from an unmounted one, so its page is conservatively kept (data-safety first) until it returns.

## User Impact

Users with `unsafe-local` notes on removable, network, or cloud-synced storage no longer lose imported pages or their manually authored human-notes when a source path (or a nested mount inside it) is briefly offline. Other healthy sources keep cleaning up deleted files normally. After the source returns, the next sync reconciles cleanly with notes intact. No config changes required.

## Evidence

### Real workflow run (standalone, non-test) — Node 24

Drove the real `syncMemoryWikiUnsafeLocalSources` from a standalone script (`node --import tsx`) against a real on-disk vault — not the test harness. The outage is a real directory `fs.rename` (ENOENT), the way an undocked drive / unmounted NAS presents. Steps per run: (1) sync imports the source page, (2) write a hand-written note into the page's human-notes block on disk, (3) take the source dir offline and sync, (4) remount and sync.

**BEFORE** — pristine base `7ac8b48a08` (current `main`):

```
[1] sync: imported=1 removed=0 page=sources/unsafe-local-notes-…-ideas-md-….md
[2] wrote user note into the page's human-notes block
[3] sync during outage: removed=1 pageStillOnDisk=false userNoteSurvived=false
[4] sync after remount: removed=0 userNoteSurvived=false

RESULT: FAIL — data was lost
```

**AFTER** — this branch, identical steps:

```
[1] sync: imported=1 removed=0 page=sources/unsafe-local-notes-…-ideas-md-….md
[2] wrote user note into the page's human-notes block
[3] sync during outage: removed=0 pageStillOnDisk=true userNoteSurvived=true
[4] sync after remount: removed=0 userNoteSurvived=true

RESULT: PASS — page + user note survived the transient outage
```

### Regression tests (real on-disk vault, pristine base vs this branch)

These regression tests drive the **real** `syncMemoryWikiUnsafeLocalSources` against a real on-disk vault, with the outage produced by a real `fs.rename` / `fs.chmod` on the source path (nothing in the collect/prune path is stubbed; only the plugin state store is the in-memory test store, which is incidental to the prune logic).

**BEFORE** — pristine base `7ac8b48a08` (current `main`), same tests, the bug reproduces:

```
 ✓ prunes stale unsafe-local pages when a file inside a readable directory is deleted
 × keeps imported pages and human notes when a configured directory is transiently unreadable
   → expected 1 to be 0   (page + human-notes were pruned/deleted)
 × does not prune when a nested subdirectory is transiently unreadable
   → expected 1 to be 0
 × keeps the imported page when an explicit configured file is transiently unavailable
   → expected 1 to be 0
 × prunes a readable source even when another configured source is unreadable
   → expected 2 to be 1   (the healthy source's page was wrongly deleted too)

 Tests  4 failed | 3 passed (7)
```

**AFTER** — this branch, identical inputs, the outage no longer destroys data:

```
 ✓ imports explicit private paths and preserves unsafe-local provenance
 ✓ prunes stale unsafe-local pages when a file inside a readable directory is deleted
 ✓ keeps imported pages and human notes when a configured directory is transiently unreadable
 ✓ does not prune when a nested subdirectory is transiently unreadable
 ✓ keeps the imported page when an explicit configured file is transiently unavailable
 ✓ prunes a readable source even when another configured source is unreadable
 ✓ caps composed unsafe-local filenames to the filesystem component limit

 Tests  7 passed (7)
```

Full verbose after-fix run — `pnpm test extensions/memory-wiki/src/unsafe-local.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`:

```
 ✓ imports explicit private paths and preserves unsafe-local provenance
 ✓ prunes stale unsafe-local pages when a file inside a readable directory is deleted
 ✓ keeps imported pages and human notes when a configured directory is transiently unreadable
 ✓ does not prune when a nested subdirectory is transiently unreadable
 ✓ keeps the imported page when an explicit configured file is transiently unavailable
 ✓ prunes a readable source even when another configured source is unreadable
 ✓ caps composed unsafe-local filenames to the filesystem component limit

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  7 passed (7)
```

What the new/updated tests prove:

- `keeps imported pages and human notes when a configured directory is transiently unreadable` — imports a page, writes a real user note into its human-notes block, takes the source directory offline with a real `fs.rename` outage, syncs (asserts `removedCount` 0 and the page + note survive), then re-mounts and syncs again (clean, note still intact).
- `does not prune when a nested subdirectory is transiently unreadable` — a nested subdirectory made unreadable mid-sync no longer prunes (`removedCount` 0).
- `keeps the imported page when an explicit configured file is transiently unavailable` — re-adds explicit-file (`unsafeLocal.paths` file shape) coverage: a vanished explicit file is preserved (`removedCount` 0, page still on disk).
- `prunes a readable source even when another configured source is unreadable` — proves the scope-aware behavior: one source offline, the other deletes a file → the healthy source still prunes (`removedCount` 1) while the offline source's page is preserved.
- `prunes stale unsafe-local pages when a file inside a readable directory is deleted` — confirms genuine deletions inside a still-readable directory still prune (`removedCount` 1).

oxlint and `oxfmt --check` are clean on both changed files.

> Note: the local full extension lane shows 4 failures in `source-sync-state.test.ts` / `doctor-contract-api.test.ts` with `TypeError: statement.columns is not a function`. These reproduce identically on a clean checkout with my changes stashed; they are a `node:sqlite` incompatibility with local Node 23 (non-LTS) and are unrelated to this change. CI runs on Linux Node 24.

AI-assisted (Claude Code); change reviewed and understood by the author.
target.author: lexes7
